## [00:00 - 03:00]

Now this is the stuff that really really excites me because this is one of the core pieces of the BPM method. This is based on over a hundred years of knowledge, experience and learning from people I followed, from books I've read, from mentors I've learned from, to carve out a simple way to understand how to pitch your product, how to build your offer, how to find your audience and what kind of funnel you need as well. So it's called the 5W Avatar. The genesis of this actually came from David Ogilvy. So you may have heard me talk about this before, sometimes I talk about them in sales pitches, sometimes in emails. Back in 1930, David Ogilvy was doing door-to-door sales and actually became a number one door-to-door salesperson, not because he was a you know hardcore closer and you know harassing people or he had some kind of you know hard sales tactics or techniques. It's simply that he understood the Avatar. The Avatar is in a given population who is who are we speaking to? You can have multiple avatars you know if you've got a gift company your avatar could be men buy for women, it could be women buy for men, it could be men for men, women for women, it could be grandchild to grandfather, you've got all so many permutations. Each and every one of those is an avatar. So it's not just the case of you know I have one avatar for my business and you know it's literally anyone that will buy gift be a bit more specific than that. So you know when we think about constructing our avatar we want to know who we are speaking to that's why it's the first part of the 5W Avatar and by the way I've highlighted the W's so you know why it's 5W's there's 5W's in this avatar sheet but that's the first part now I'm not going to go into the detail of how to construct this this will come later on when we talk about offering audience building for our advertising and later on in the BPM method but I want to highlight the importance of this for your products and in particular it's Maslow's hierarchy of needs that we focus on when we want to figure out where does our product sit and how are we going to actually get ready for ads and make sure that our offers are creatives are landers everything is primed for the human on the other side and this is something which I think a lot of advertisers forget over time is yes you're building things into ad platforms and you're looking at clicks and visuals and graphics and videos landing pages funnels all that kind of stuff at the end of the day you're serving an ad or an email or a piece of content to a human on the other side the human on the other side is at times irrational and unpredictable but there are other things that are quite predictable so what Maslow's hierarchy of needs does is to break down those predictabilities into

## [03:00 - 06:04]

basic needs and think of this as stacking up so think about this for yourself or for your prospect everyone has the most basic needs of food water sleep and other things that allow us to operate as humans then we have safety needs so do I have money do I have a house am I in an environment that's safe are there police around if I need it you know all those kind of things that make you feel secure then there's you know relationships love and friendship which are the layer above that which is easier to strive for once you have safety and your basic needs met and then there's achievement respect confidence things that start to build your character and right the top of the hierarchy is the higher purpose am I having an impact in life am I giving money to charity am I helping fellow humans am I doing something I really enjoy am I really expanding my horizons and going to holidays things like that so the way I look at this is where does my product sit on my Maslow's hierarchy of needs so let's say for example we're selling a bottle of water it's the most basic needs for every single human on the planet to have water do I need to explain to someone the benefit of having water I'd probably say most people know they need to drink water in fact if they don't know that and that's a problem so therefore in my advertising I don't need to explain what water is what I do need to explain is why am I selling water if I can go into the local store and buy water why am I selling on water online it could be and this is where it comes down to carving out your product and I'm totally making this up I don't even know if this exists but let's say for example you're in a keto diet and this water has a special blend of minerals and nutrients that is that powers your keto diet now all of a sudden we're talking about something else we're not talking about water we're going up the hierarchy we're not talking about safety or level belonging we're probably talking about esteem so why then I go into that and say right why would someone be interested in my water okay it's a keto water why someone interested in keto they want to lose weight they want to build muscle they want to improve their gut health and all these things start to come out which are linked to esteem confidence achievement now I'm thinking right how do I pitch for that so we've moved down from the rational moved up from the rational part which is everyone knows I need water up to the emotional part do you want to lose weight do you want to maintain that weight off do you want to grow muscle mass all those kind of things it becomes easier to sell when you're selling emotionally because we are emotional beings it's also harder for us to rationalize what the proposition is when we talk about emotions so one of the examples I always talk about which is the I think the perfect

## [06:04 - 09:05]

example to illustrate this is I think 2016 2017 time LED dog collars were a really really big thing now if you look at LED dog collars they kind of fell into safety so they kept your dog safe and seen selling on those features but the ads that really stuck out moved it from safety to love and belonging just by changing the pitch so safety is a very rational thing so if I said to you I've got this LED dog collar it will keep your dog safe and things like that would you pay me $30 now you're thinking rationally $30 for this piece of plastic with a light and a battery maybe maybe I'm not sure I maybe have a think about I'll get back to you now let's shift it up to love and belonging this LED dog collar could save your dog's life now if I say to you how much would you pay for that now you're not thinking about batteries and plastic and things like that we're focusing on your pet there's no price that you could probably come up with that says I'm you know my pets worth this because because end of it and this is you know this is a dark art of marketing now someone's going to be really keen to make sure that doesn't happen again or if it doesn't happen hasn't happened to them they don't
